goal: From this nginx access log, find the IP that issued the most 4xx-status requests. Print 'IP\tCOUNT'.
From this CSV (header 'date,category,amount'), output the top 3 categories by total amount, formatted as 'CATEGORY: $TOTAL', one per line, descending.
From this Python source (newline-separated), extract just the names of every top-level (no leading whitespace) `def NAME(`. Print the names, one per line, in order of appearance.
Take a JSON object of the form {"users":[{"name":..., "role":...}, ...]}. Print the names of users whose role is 'admin', one per line, in input order.
Convert this 3-column TSV (with header) to a Markdown table with a header divider row. Use ' | ' as separator and pipe-padded edges.
input:
name	age	city
alice	30	paris
bob	25	nyc
expected:
| name | age | city |
| --- | --- | --- |
| alice | 30 | paris |
| bob | 25 | nyc |

goal: From this log (lines like 'YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS LEVEL message'), find the HOUR (HH only) with the most ERROR-level entries. Print just that 2-digit hour.
input:
2026-05-01T08:14:01 INFO startup
2026-05-01T09:22:05 ERROR connection refused
2026-05-01T09:25:12 ERROR timeout
2026-05-01T09:48:33 INFO user login
2026-05-01T09:51:01 ERROR connection refused
2026-05-01T10:01:11 WARN slow query
2026-05-01T11:33:22 ERROR timeout
2026-05-01T11:34:00 INFO restart

goal: Extract every dotted-quad IPv4 address from arg0 and print one per line in order of appearance.
From access-log lines like 'GET /path 200 142', print 'PATH STATUS' for each line.
